exemplar.title: An investigation into the effect of font type and colour on number of correct responses on the Cognitive Reflection Test

detailedExemplarData.title: An investigation into the effect of font type and colour on number of correct responses on the Cognitive Reflection Test

subject.title: Psychology
subject.description:
IB Psychology is a Group 3 subject in the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP). The course examines human behavior and mental processes through scientific inquiry, exploring biological, cognitive, and sociocultural approaches to understanding behavior. Students develop critical thinking skills while investigating contemporary psychological research, research methods, and ethical considerations in psychological practice.

The course is available at Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours) and Higher Level (HL, 240 hours), with first assessment in 2027.

Assessment consists of Paper 1 (integration of concepts, content, and contexts through short-answer and extended-response questions: SL 2 hours, HL 2 hours, SL 50%, HL 40%), Paper 2 (application of psychological knowledge to research contexts: SL 1 hour, HL 2 hours, SL 25%, HL 20%), Paper 3 (HL only: research methods and data analysis, 1 hour, 20%), and Internal Assessment (research proposal development: SL 25%, HL 20%).

strengths: Clear, focused aim with explicit theoretical and real-world relevance
Accurate description of the Dual Process Model and explicit linkage to the investigation
Hypotheses include clearly operationalized independent and dependent variables
weaknesses: Minor stylistic wordiness in the aim statement
Could specify age range and language proficiency for participant population
Directional nature of the hypothesis could be more explicitly stated
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands meet the 5â6 band descriptors: the aim is stated and its relevance is explained; the theory/model is described and linked; and variables are operationalized. Minor stylistic issues keep it at a solid 5 rather than a perfect 6.

strengths: Independent-measures design is clearly explained with justification
Participant sampling technique and characteristics are described and reasons provided
Controlled variables and materials are explained with clear links to validity
weaknesses: Could discuss generalizability limits of convenience sampling
More detail on the delivery of standardized instructions would enhance replicability
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands achieve the top Exploration band. The design, sampling, and control of variables are fully explained, meriting the maximum 4.

strengths: Correct use of descriptive and inferential statistics (Mann-Whitney U)
Graph correctly presented, labeled, and addressing the hypothesis
Findings are linked to hypotheses and interpreted in context
weaknesses: Statistical language imprecise (U-value vs. p-value conflation)
Table header missing for one column, reducing clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Statistical methods and presentation are appropriate but minor interpretive and formatting errors prevent a higher mark. This justifies a 4 in Analysis.

strengths: Discussion ties findings back to the Dual Process Model
Strengths and limitations are clearly explained and relevant to validity
Modifications are explicitly linked to limitations and justified
weaknesses: Could include power analysis or sample-size justification
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Evaluation shows reasoned discussion, well-linked strengths/limitations, and justified modifications, but minor gaps in the limitations section and absence of a power discussion limit it to a 4.

strandSelections.reasoning: A bar graph is provided comparing mean correct answers across conditions. Axes are labelled, units and title are present, and the graph directly addresses the hypothesis. No substantive graphical errors are evident, so the work meets the âcorrectly presentedâ descriptor. A mid-band (5) score is appropriate because the graph is clear but basic.

The student states numerical findings, interprets them relative to both descriptive trends and the Mann-Whitney outcome, and explicitly links conclusions back to the null and research hypotheses. This dual reference to data and hypothesis satisfies the top-band descriptor. Minor statistical language imprecision prevents a perfect 6, so a 5 is awarded.

Both null and research hypotheses are stated; the IV (font legibility: easy vs difficult) and DV (number of correct answers out of 3) are not only identified but **clearly operationalised**. The method of scoring (1 or 0 per item, mean per condition) shows explicit operationalisation. Hence the work meets the top-band descriptor; minor wording imperfections do not lower the quality, warranting a 5.

Participant characteristics (age, gender balance, English fluency, non-psychology students) are outlined and **reasons for these choices** are given (avoid confounds, ensure comprehension). Convenience sampling is identified and its practical rationale provided. This constitutes an explanation rather than mere description, fitting the 3-4 band; a 4 is appropriate because the justification is clear though not exhaustive.

The Dual Process Model is **accurately described**: characteristics of System 1 and System 2 are laid out and sources are cited. The student then **explicitly connects** the model to the planned manipulation (easy- vs difficult-to-read fonts) and the expected patterns of correct/incorrect CRT answers. This goes beyond mere identification, fully satisfying the âdescribed and link explainedâ requirement. Small factual slips (e.g., equating grey colour with illegibility) are negligible, so a score of 5 in the top band is justified.

The discussion revisits the Dual Process Model, ties mean differences to System 1 vs System 2 engagement, and integrates the non-significant inferential result. This constitutes a **reasoned discussion**, not just description. Depth is good though briefly presented, meriting a 5 within the highest band.

The student states a clear, focused aim: to investigate the effect of font legibility on the number of correct CRT answers among high-school students. Immediately after, the relevance is **explicitly explained** in terms of how manipulating legibility may foster System 2 processing and thus help students study more deeply. Because both the aim and its real-world/ theoretical relevance are not only identified but also **logically justified**, the work fits the 5-6 band. Minor stylistic issues (e.g., wordiness) do not detract from the substantive explanation, so a band-mid score of 5 is appropriate.

Multiple strengths (independent measures, standardised instructions, exclusion of psychology students) and limitations (participant effort, ecological validity, convenience sampling) are **explained in relation to validity and reliability**. Each point is clearly linked to how it influences results, meeting the âexplained and relevantâ criterion. Coverage is thorough though not exhaustive, justifying a 5.

Multiple controlled variables (testing alone, fixed time, standardised instructions) are listed **with cogent explanations** of how each prevents confounds. Materials (specific fonts, size, colour) are also justified. This meets the âexplainedâ criterion, so the highest Exploration band is reached; a score of 4 reflects solid but not exceptionally detailed coverage.

For each major limitation, the student proposes a **specific, justified modification** (effort-rating item, more ecologically valid reading task, random sampling). The proposals are explicitly tied back to the stated issues, fully satisfying the top-band descriptor. While succinct, the linkage and justification are clear, warranting a 5.

The student describes an independent-measures design **and explains** why it was chosen (avoiding order effects and demand characteristics). Random allocation is mentioned as further justification, fulfilling the âexplainedâ requirement. The explanation is complete though not deeply analytical, meriting the top band for Exploration but the lower end (4) due to brevity.

Descriptive statistics (means, SDs) are correctly calculated; inferential statistics use an appropriate non-parametric test (Mann-Whitney U) for independent-measures ratio data. The decision rule (observed U > critical U â retain H0) is correctly applied. Minor phrasing such as â27 % chanceâ is imprecise but does not alter the conclusion. Overall application is accurate, warranting placement in the 5-6 band; a 5 recognises very good accuracy with minor wording flaws.

annotations.comment: Participant characteristics and exclusion of psychology students are well justified, effectively reducing potential confounds.
Discuss how convenience sampling limits generalisability and consider potential biases introduced by selecting participants based on availability.
Clear explanation of the independent-measures design and rationale (avoiding order effects and bias) demonstrates sound research design.
Multiple controls (environment, timing, instructions) are clearly explained and directly linked to maintaining validity of the IV manipulation.
Correctly identifies data level as ratio and chooses mean and SD appropriately, reflecting sound descriptive statistical reasoning.
Table lacks a header for the first column (e.g. âStatisticâ); adding it will improve clarity and ease of interpretation.
Interpretation equates larger SD with reduced reliability; clarify that SD describes variability, not reliability of measurement.
Graph is correctly presented with clear labels, units, and caption; it directly addresses the research hypothesis.
States support for hypothesis based on descriptive data before inferential analysis; separate descriptive and inferential conclusions for clarity.
Include details on how standardised instructions were delivered (e.g., read verbatim) to enhance replicability of the procedure.
Rationale for font choice relies on subjective cursive resemblance; strengthen by citing studies on legibility effects.
Misinterprets U-value as percentage chance; distinguish between the Mann-Whitney U statistic and its associated p-value for accurate inference.
Effectively links findings to the Dual Process Model by discussing how font legibility influenced System 1 and System 2 engagement.
Identifies and explains methodological strengths (e.g., standardised instructions, replicable CRT), demonstrating relevance to reliability.
Limitation discussion is cut off mid-sentence; ensure the full explanation of its impact on validity is clearly articulated.
Acknowledges participant effort variability and proposes an effort-rating scale, directly addressing a key design limitation.
Addresses ecological validity by suggesting realistic study materials, improving applicability of findings to real-world contexts.
Consider conducting a power analysis or increasing sample size to address limited generalisability and enhance statistical validity.
Conclusion could note the limited power of a 20-participant sample to contextualise the non-significant result more appropriately.
Title mentions colour but the colour effect is not discussed in the methodology; consider aligning the title with the variables actually manipulated.
The table of contents lacks page numbers, which hinders navigation; include specific page references for each section and appendix.
Accurately describes System 1 and System 2 characteristics with sources, demonstrating a solid understanding of the Dual Process Model.
Brief mention of Alter et al. could include more methodological detail (e.g., sample, procedures) to strengthen the theoretical link to the font manipulation.
Consider operationalising the âhigh-school student populationâ in the aim (e.g. age range, language proficiency) to achieve greater precision.
Independent variable is clearly defined and operationalised (font type, colour, size), satisfying top-band criteria for hypothesis operationalisation.
Dependent variable is explicitly operationalised by the scoring method (1/0 per question and averaging), ensuring clarity and measurability.
Hypothesis would benefit from specifying its directional (one-tailed) nature explicitly to align with the inferential testing strategy.
